layer_definitions.COGNITION: Transforms human intent into structured understanding without creating authority.
layer_definitions.RESOURCE_SELECTION: Chooses an eligible Resource and explicit active role without invoking, authorizing, or executing it.
layer_definitions.PPP: Turns structured intent and selected Resources into proposal, validation, approval, repair, and handoff artifacts without becoming governance or execution.
layer_definitions.GOVERNANCE: Determines admissibility, approval, authorization, and certification while preserving constitutional and replay boundaries.
layer_definitions.EXECUTION: Performs only explicitly authorized bounded work and returns replay-visible result evidence.

drift_findings: PPP is the most drift-prone layer because it touches cognition artifacts, resource selection artifacts, provider proposal production, proposal validation, approval surfacing, and handoff.
Provider necessity and resource selection must remain outside PPP decision ownership.
Proposal validation must not be treated as governance approval.
Implementation handoff must not be treated as execution request or dispatch.
Hybrid Resources must not switch roles implicitly.
certification_result: AIGOL architectural layer boundaries are certified with PPP preserved as a proposal pipeline and explicit drift guardrails.
